title: Total number of civilians killed during the siege of Budapest
strengths: Explicitly distinguishes broader mixed mortality from non-disease categories and explains why the common 38,000 figure is overstated for siege-only civilian deaths.
limitations: Open access here is via an online summary/extract rather than the formatted paper itself; the exact treatment of present-day suburban municipalities remains somewhat ambiguous.
notes: Core source for the civilian direct-death path.

title: Budaörs German-Hungarian War Cemetery flyer
strengths: Puts the city population and both sides' dead into one compact Budapest-focused summary.
limitations: Memorial brochure rather than a formal scholarly study; attacker losses are rounded ('almost 80,000').
notes: Used for city population and military death totals.

title: Hungarian Nightmare
author_or_org: Mark Stille / Against the Odds Magazine
strengths: Provides a compact city-battle force comparison and dates the encircled combat phase explicitly.
limitations: Secondary summary in a rules introduction rather than a dedicated scholarly operational study.

title: The Siege of Budapest
strengths: Widely cited Budapest history and useful for identifying the older 38,000 civilian-death figure.
limitations: The 38,000 civilian figure is explicitly rejected here for the core direct-death path because it conflates direct and indirect mortality.
notes: Retained as contextual and rejected-source review material, not as the core civilian death estimate.
